whyItMatters: Claims require a rendering provider. Without at least one active provider, no encounters can be billed.
resolution: Add at least one active provider on the Providers settings page.

message: No active providers can bill independently.
whyItMatters: Only providers flagged as billing-independent can appear as the rendering provider on a claim. Without one, claims will be rejected.
resolution: Mark at least one active provider as able to bill independently.

message: One or more active providers are missing an NPI.
whyItMatters: Every rendering provider on a claim must have a 10-digit NPI. Claims for providers without an NPI will be rejected at the clearinghouse.
resolution: Add the NPI to each provider listed in evidence on the Providers settings page.

message: One or more provider NPIs fail the Luhn check digit validation.
whyItMatters: An NPI with a bad check digit is not a valid identifier. Claims will be rejected and eligibility lookups will fail.
resolution: Correct the NPI for each provider listed in evidence. Verify against NPPES (https://npiregistry.cms.hhs.gov/).

message: One or more active providers are missing a taxonomy code.
whyItMatters: The provider taxonomy code is required on 837P claims (PRV segment) and is used by payers to confirm the provider's specialty. Missing taxonomy causes rejections.
resolution: Add the NUCC taxonomy code for each provider listed in evidence. See https://taxonomy.nucc.org/.
